task_completion.description: A Line markup is created in the scene
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Line markup is visible in the 3D Slicer viewer (either in the 3D view or 2D slice views).
The Markups module or Data module shows the Line markup explicitly named 'Biopsy_Trajectory'.

task_completion.description: The markup consists of exactly two control points
task_completion.visual_evidence: The control points list in the Markups module shows exactly two points defining the line.

task_completion.description: The entry point is located on the scalp superior to the tumor
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 3D or 2D views, one end of the line clearly originates at the outer surface of the head (scalp) and is positioned superiorly (above) relative to the tumor.

task_completion.description: The target point terminates inside the tumor region
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D slice views, the second end of the line terminates within the visible tumor mass, corresponding to the coordinates from the target text file.

task_completion.description: The trajectory length is between 30 and 120 mm
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Markups module displays the length of the line, and the value is between 30 mm and 120 mm.
The 'Save Data' dialog is visible and shows the scene being saved, or there is a terminal/file explorer window showing the newly saved scene file.

integrity.description: The agent used the 3D Slicer GUI to create the markup
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 3D Slicer interface with the Markups module open and mouse interactions (placing points), rather than just a terminal executing a script to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent read the tumor_target.txt file to get the coordinates
integrity.visual_evidence: A terminal window, text editor, or Python console is visible at some point showing the contents of /home/ga/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/tumor_target.txt.

integrity.description: The agent did not load a pre-computed solution
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the step-by-step creation of the markup (e.g., placing the entry and target points sequentially) rather than the final markup appearing instantly in the first few steps.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected markup name is exactly 'Biopsy_Trajectory'. It must be a Line markup with exactly 2 control points. The valid length of the trajectory is between 30 mm and 120 mm. The target point must be within a 15.0 mm tolerance of the coordinates specified in the tumor_target.txt file. The entry point must be on the scalp superior to the tumor.
